At least 14 people were killed and five injured after a minibus lost control on a curve and veered off a mountain road in the Peruvian Andes, with rescue crews using ropes to recover victims from the steep hillside.

Oil prices jumped 10% this week as fighting over the Strait of Hormuz escalated, with Iranian state media reporting at least seven people killed and 20 wounded in the latest US strikes, and Iranian health officials saying 38 people have been killed and over 400 wounded in the past week.

Andy Burnham becomes Prime Minister on Monday and has told Scottish Labour MPs he will maintain the UK government's opposition to a second independence referendum, taking a firmer line than he gave in previous years.

Russia launched 41 missiles and 125 attack drones across Ukraine overnight, and Ukraine's navy said five people were killed when a Turkish-owned civilian cargo ship carrying grain was hit by Russian missiles in the Black Sea.
